# Research — 2026-05-29

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents)

> Second pass on 2026-05-29. The morning pass covered claude-code v2.1.147–v2.1.156 and cline up to May 27.
> This revision integrates new findings from May 28–29 commits (cline #11103, #11113, #11115; kilocode #10658)
> and corrects two prior items where the gap was misdiagnosed (we already have `disabledTools` on skill schema —
> the gap is enforcement, not the field; and `fallbackModel` is in routing config but never honored at the provider boundary).

## Executive Summary

- **4 of 5 items from 2026-05-19 are now closed** in our codebase: paginated MCP `tools/list` (`src/mcp/client.ts:64-76`, `MAX_PAGES=100`), startup connectivity timeout (`src/providers/connectivity.ts` + `StartupTimeoutError`), `/rewind` and "Summarize up to here" (`src/command/rewind.ts`, `src/cli/tui/hooks/useRewind.ts`), and prompt-history consecutive-duplicate suppression (`src/cli/tui/components/InputBox.tsx:222`).
- **2 items remain open from 2026-05-19**: goal-driven autonomous loop (`/goal`) and per-`execute()` agent usage reset.
- **anthropics/claude-code v2.1.156** (May 28): hotfix only — Opus 4.8 thinking-block API errors. v2.1.154 (May 28) shipped Opus 4.8 default-high effort, **dynamic workflows** (`/workflows`, orchestrating tens-to-hundreds of background agents), the lean system prompt as default (everything except Haiku/Sonnet/Opus 4.7-), and context-aware question reservation.
- **claude-code v2.1.154 — `claude --bg --exec '<command>'`**: a one-shot way to launch a background shell as a tracked agent session (and `! <cmd>` inside `claude agents`). Bridges the gap between "run a command" and "run an agent."
- **claude-code v2.1.154 — plugin `defaultEnabled: false`**: plugins ship installed-but-disabled by default; `/plugin` or `claude plugin enable` flips them on. Dependencies of an enabled plugin are still pulled in automatically. We have plugin enable/disable infra (`src/plugin/index.ts:821-925`) but no notion of `defaultEnabled` at install time.
- **claude-code v2.1.149 — PowerShell `cd..`/`cd~`/`X:` permission bypass fix**: Bash variants like `cd ..`, `pushd`, and parenthesized subshells can mutate the working directory undetected. Worth auditing our `src/permission/` parser even though we don't run PowerShell — the same class of bug affects POSIX shells.
- **cline #11103 (May 28) — global AGENTS rules in the SDK**: cline now resolves a **global** (user-level) AGENTS file in addition to project AGENTS.md, and classifies each rule by exact path so duplicates from project + global are de-duped. We load `~/.alexi/ALEXI.md` (`src/agent/system.ts:165`) but **not** `~/AGENTS.md` or `~/.config/AGENTS.md`. AGENTS.md has emerged as a community-standard filename — supporting it globally is one of those low-effort, high-leverage parity items.
- **cline #11113 (May 29) — symlinked SDK skill directory discovery**: their loader silently skipped skills installed via symlink (the common pattern when a workspace pulls in skills from another checkout, or when a package manager places them through symlinks). They added explicit `realpath`/`lstat` traversal with circular-symlink protection. Our skill loader should be checked for the same regression.
- **cline #11115 — connector turn-key steering** and #11114 (May 28) — Discord author-bound sessions: out of scope for our SAP-AI-Core CLI envelope. Tracked here only so future research doesn't re-flag them.
- **kilocode #10658 (May 29)**: removed legacy `Provider.list/getModel/...` promise wrappers in favor of Effect `Provider.Service.use()` DI. Internal refactor — relevant only if/when we adopt an Effect-style runtime; no user-facing feature.
- **Two prior gaps were misdiagnosed** in the morning pass and are corrected below: (a) `disallowed-tools` frontmatter is **already in the SkillSchema** as `disabledTools`, but `streamingOrchestrator.ts` never applies it for active skills; (b) `fallbackModel` exists in `routingConfig` but is consulted only by the score-based router, never by the provider boundary on a model-not-found error.

## Detailed Findings

### What was built since 2026-05-19 (closed gaps)
- MCP `nextCursor` loop in `src/mcp/client.ts:64-76` with `MAX_PAGES=100` cap — closes pagination gap.
- `src/providers/connectivity.ts` + `StartupTimeoutError` — closes the 75s startup hang.
- `src/command/rewind.ts` + `useRewind.ts` — closes "Summarize up to here."
- `InputBox.tsx:222` consecutive-dup suppression — closes prompt-history dedup.

### Still open from 2026-05-19
- **`/goal` autonomous loop** — no command, no evaluator. `src/core/orchestrator.ts` is single-turn.
- **Per-`execute()` agent usage reset** — `src/agent/index.ts` accumulates usage across dispatches (cline v3.0.7 fix).

### New gaps (sorted by leverage)

#### 1. Global AGENTS.md resolution (Category: config — small, < 4h)
- **What**: Resolve a user-level AGENTS file in addition to the existing project `AGENTS.md` and `~/.alexi/ALEXI.md`. Search order: `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/AGENTS.md` → `~/.config/AGENTS.md` → `~/AGENTS.md` (first hit wins). De-dup by absolute resolved path so a project that symlinks `~/AGENTS.md` into the workspace doesn't get loaded twice.
- **Why**: cline #11103 (May 28) made this an SDK-level feature precisely because community tools have converged on `AGENTS.md` as the canonical agent-rules filename. Our `src/agent/system.ts:155-188` loads project `AGENTS.md` and user `~/.alexi/ALEXI.md` but the latter is a project-private name and many users will have already authored a global `AGENTS.md` for other tools (Cursor, claude-code, cline, codex, etc.). Honoring it costs ~30 lines and removes a friction point for new users.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11103 `feat(sdk): support global AGENTS rules` (May 28).
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

#### 2. Enforce skill `disabledTools` at runtime (Category: tool — small, < 4h)
- **What**: `SkillSchema` in `src/skill/index.ts:34` already declares `disabledTools: z.array(z.string()).optional()`. The agent runtime (`src/agent/subagent-permissions.ts:61`) honors it for agents, but `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts` never filters the model's tool list when a skill is active. Add a step in the streaming orchestrator that, for the duration of a turn where one or more skills are active (already tracked by the active-skill resolver), removes their union of `disabledTools` from the tools array passed to the provider.
- **Why**: claude-code v2.1.152 shipped this as `disallowed-tools` frontmatter; we have the schema field but no enforcement, which is worse than not having it at all (silent permission gap, skill authors think they're constraining behavior). Smallest possible safety win.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.152 + existing schema in `src/skill/index.ts:34`.
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

#### 3. `getProviderForModel` fallback on `Unknown model` (Category: provider — small, < 4h)
- **What**: When `getProviderForModel(modelId)` would throw `Unknown model`, or the API returns a model-not-found 4xx for the configured deployment, automatically retry with `routingConfig.preferences.fallbackModel` (`src/config/routingConfig.ts:97`) and pin the fallback for the rest of the session. Surface a single one-line warning in the TUI status area: `model X unavailable → using Y for this session`.
- **Why**: Today the score-based router (`src/core/router.ts:207`) reads `fallbackModel`, but every direct provider call (chat, agent dispatch, summarize) bypasses it. A deployment-id typo or a renamed SAP deployment breaks every turn until the user restarts. claude-code v2.1.152 ships this exact behavior. Highest reliability-per-LOC of any item in the queue.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.152.
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

#### 4. Symlink-aware skill discovery (Category: tool — small, < 2h)
- **What**: Audit `src/skill/skills/index.ts` and the skill scanner in `src/skill/index.ts` to ensure they `realpath` directory entries and traverse through symlinks (with a `Set<string>` of resolved paths to break cycles). Add a regression test that creates a symlinked skill dir under a temp `workdir` and asserts it loads.
- **Why**: cline #11113 (May 29) shipped exactly this fix because skills installed via package managers, monorepo symlinks, or hand-managed shared directories were silently skipped. Cheap to verify; if we already handle it, the test still has value.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11113 `fix: discover symlinked SDK skill directories` (May 29).
- **Size**: small (< 2h)

#### 5. `/code-review [effort]` command (Category: command — medium, 1-2 days) — *carry-over from morning pass*
- **What**: A `/code-review [effort]` command running a structured correctness-bug review over uncommitted changes (or `git diff base...HEAD`) at a chosen effort level. `--fix` applies safe simplification/efficiency suggestions; `--comment` posts findings as inline GitHub PR comments via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{N}/comments`.
- **Why**: claude-code v2.1.147 renamed `/simplify` → `/code-review`; v2.1.152 added `--fix`; v2.1.154 split them so `/simplify` does cleanup-only and `/code-review --fix` does the bug hunt — this is now their flagship review flow. We have a `code-review` skill (`src/skill/skills/index.ts:11`) but no command bundling diff selection, effort tuning, fix application, and PR-comment posting.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.147–v2.1.154.
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days). Split into two PRs: (a) command + diff selection + effort plumbing reusing the existing skill prompt; (b) `--comment` GitHub integration.

### Notable upstream items deliberately deferred
- **Opus 4.8 / dynamic workflows / `/workflows`**: model rollout is upstream-driven and dynamic workflows is an Anthropic-managed orchestration product (hundreds of background agents, fast-mode pricing) — outside our SAP-AI-Core, single-binary CLI envelope. Track via `src/config/routingConfig.ts:42` when SAP exposes the deployment.
- **`MessageDisplay` hook (v2.1.152)**: no message-mutation use cases in our codebase yet. Re-evaluate when skill authors start asking for redaction/privacy hooks.
- **`claude --bg --exec '<command>'` and `! <cmd>` (v2.1.154)**: we already have `background_process` tool and `bash` tool — adding a one-shot CLI shortcut is sugar, not a capability gap. Defer until we have a request.
- **Plugin `defaultEnabled: false` (v2.1.154)**: relevant only if/when we publish a plugin marketplace. Our plugin loader (`src/plugin/index.ts`) uses on-demand activation already; no user-facing problem to solve.
- **PowerShell `cd..` permission bypass (v2.1.149)**: not directly applicable (we don't run PowerShell), but the underlying class — built-in shell forms that mutate `PWD` outside the parser's variable tracker — could affect `src/permission/` for POSIX `cd ..`, `pushd`, `cd -`, and `(cd /tmp && cmd)` subshells. **Action**: open a follow-up issue to audit, do not block on this cycle.
- **kilocode #10658 Effect refactor**: internal-only. No action.

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Close the carry-overs first** (`/goal`, per-execute usage reset). They were already specified, scoped, and source-cited in `2026-05-19-research.md`. Closing them keeps research-to-implementation latency honest and unblocks the autonomous-agent stories already in the backlog.
2. **Ship the three small wins in one cycle** (each < 4h, ~1 day total):
   - Item #3: `getProviderForModel` fallback — biggest reliability gain.
   - Item #2: enforce skill `disabledTools` — closes a silent permission gap that is already half-implemented.
   - Item #1: global AGENTS.md — high-visibility parity with cline/claude-code/cursor.
3. **Item #4 (symlinked skills)** can ride along — a 30-minute audit + one regression test. Treat as a chore PR.
4. **Item #5 (`/code-review` command)** is the medium-effort feature for this cycle. Two-PR split as above. The `--comment` PR needs a `gh` CLI dependency check (`src/utils/gh.ts` doesn't exist yet — add one and reuse from any future `/pr-status`-style commands).
5. **Open a tracking issue** for the POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell permission audit (deferred from PowerShell finding) and link it from `src/permission/` so the next maintainer touching that area sees it.
6. **Re-evaluate `MessageDisplay` and `--bg --exec`** next cycle if usage reports surface.
